commit.message:
refactor: code quality improvements and security hardening

- ai-engine/diagnoser.py: remove /tmp/ollama_available file-based detection
  (unreliable, only OLLAMA_BASE_URL env var is supported); separate provider
  selection from API call in diagnose() so model_used='error' is set correctly
  when an API call fails (was incorrectly returning 'none')

- ai-engine/analyzers: wrap analyze() in try/except in OpenAI and Gemini
  analyzers to raise RuntimeError on API failures; catch httpx.HTTPStatusError
  and httpx.RequestError in OllamaAnalyzer.analyze() to give callers a clear
  error signal instead of an unhandled exception

- backend/controllers/yaml_controller.py: add 512 KB size validation to the
  /yaml/diff endpoint (was only enforced on /yaml/scan), preventing DoS via
  oversized payloads

- backend/utils.py: expand SENSITIVE_PATTERNS to also mask JSON/YAML inline
  secrets ("password":"value"), database URLs with embedded passwords
  (postgres://user:pass@host), and AWS access key IDs (AKIA...)

- backend/services/yaml_service.py: replace ANTI_PATTERN_RULES list[dict] with
  a typed AntiPatternRule frozen dataclass for type-safety and IDE support

- tests: 79 tests (+9 new):
  * test_diagnoser: add coverage for /tmp/ollama_available removal, OpenAI API
    error fallback, Gemini API error fallback, OllamaAnalyzer HTTP/request
    error handling
  * test_endpoints: add test for diff endpoint size validation (422 on oversize)
  * test_utils: add tests for database URL masking, AWS key masking, JSON inline
    secret masking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add SQLite persistence for chat threads and messages

 (#4)

* Add SQLite persistence for chat threads and messages

Implement a full persistence layer using better-sqlite3 so chat history
survives page refreshes. DB location is environment-aware:
- Local dev: ~/.optopsy/db/optopsy.db
- Railway prod: $RAILWAY_VOLUME_MOUNT_PATH/db/optopsy.db (or $DB_PATH)

New files:
- lib/db.ts — SQLite schema (threads + messages tables) and CRUD operations
- lib/sqlite-chat-runtime.tsx — Custom RemoteThreadListAdapter and
  ThreadHistoryAdapter that call API routes for persistence, replacing
  the default in-memory/cloud adapter from assistant-ui
- app/api/threads/ — REST API routes for thread list, thread CRUD,
  and message load/save

The adapter integrates with assistant-ui's unstable_useRemoteThreadListRuntime
and provides a ThreadHistoryAdapter via RuntimeAdapterProvider so messages
are automatically persisted when runs complete.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017mX8LQwryiDajFrVjAJtBC

* Address SQLite persistence layer review feedback (#5)

* Initial plan

* Address PR review feedback: db, runtime adapter, and package fixes

Co-authored-by: michaelchu <540510+michaelchu@users.noreply.github.com>

Expand 05_macros.py with comprehensive preprocessor demo

Demonstrates all macro features added in prior sessions:
- Object-like (#define NL, SEP, MAX_BYTE) and function-like macros
- $param substitution: DOUBLE, SWAP macros
- Backslash line continuation: CLAMP_MAX multi-line definition
- Nested macro expansion: TRIPLE calls DOUBLE
- Shift operators (<<, >>) via SHL/SHR macros
- #ifdef / #else / #endif with #undef teardown
- #ifndef include-guard (prevents double-definition of UTIL_LABEL)
- #if 0 / #if 1 dead-code and always-on blocks
- #elif chained condition

Also updates examples/README.md to describe the expanded demo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: remove archived task files

Clean up 550 completed/archived task files from .vscode/tasks/archive/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(fuzzer): normalize devNNN/memNNN IDs in QMP error messages

Device and object IDs are echoed verbatim in QEMU error responses:
  Device 'dev42' not found
  object 'mem99' not found

With 256 possible numeric suffixes per ID, each distinct b1/b2 argument byte
produced a unique stdout hash for the same logical outcome, keeping the
corpus add-rate at 100% indefinitely.

Add 'dev and 'mem to DIRECT_PATTERNS with a digit-presence guard so
'device' and 'developer' are not incorrectly normalised.  Device IDs
now consistently hash as 'dev0' / 'mem0' across all argument values.

This reduced the corpus add-rate from 100% to ~65% at 24 s and is
expected to plateau well below 50% as the behavioral space saturates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: Implement comprehensive improvements to KnowIt

CRITICAL IMPROVEMENTS (Tier 1):
- Enable code minification (R8) and resource shrinking for release builds
- Add test infrastructure: JUnit, MockK, Coroutines test, Compose UI tests
- Implement high score persistence using Android DataStore
- Add state persistence using SavedStateHandle for configuration changes
- Add full accessibility support (WCAG 2.1 Level A):
  * contentDescription on all UI elements and icons
  * Semantic labels for interactive components
  * Screen reader compatible throughout

HIGH PRIORITY IMPROVEMENTS (Tier 2):
- Refactor GameScreen.kt from 581 lines → 7 composable components:
  * GameScreenTopBar.kt (score, streak, question counter)
  * QuestionCard.kt (question display with category)
  * AnswerOptions.kt (multiple choice buttons)
  * TypeInAnswerInput.kt (text input with submit)
  * AnswerFeedback.kt (correct/wrong feedback animations)
  * ConfettiAnimation.kt (particle effects)
  * CategoryProgressBar.kt (progress visualization)
- Improve answer validation: normalize whitespace, strip punctuation
- Add null safety checks throughout ViewModel
- Extract duplicated scoring logic into reusable function
- Fix release workflow to build release APK instead of debug
- Create GameViewModelFactory for dependency injection

TESTING & CI/CD:
- Create GameViewModelTest with 11 comprehensive test cases
- Create QuestionBankValidationTest with 10 validation tests
- Enhance build.yml CI pipeline: add test execution, upload test results
- Fix release.yml to build production-ready release APK

CODE QUALITY & DOCUMENTATION:
- Add extensive KDoc comments to ViewModel, enums, and data classes
- Improve ProGuard rules: targeted keeps, remove debug logging
- Add HighScoreRepository for clean data layer abstraction
- Update build.gradle.kts dependencies (DataStore, test libraries)

MAINTENANCE:
- Add 13 test dependencies to version catalog
- Ensure backward compatibility (zero breaking changes)
- All animations preserved and optimized
- State management improved with SavedStateHandle

Files Created: 10
Files Modified: 11
Lines Added: 1513
Lines Removed: 466

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix diagram consistency and upgrade references in file storage

Diagram fixes:
- Notification long-polling diagram: replace opaque cursors (c_42,
  c_43) with change_seq journal model (98234, 98235), matching the
  sync_changes table and API design
- Multi-device sync diagram: replace c_30/c_50/c_51 with change_seq
  values (98230/98250/98251), show tombstone for delete event
- High-level architecture diagram: add Governance & Security layer
  with Malware/DLP Scanner, Audit Log, and Legal Hold Service;
  show scan queue, verdict, audit event, and hold check flows
  with dashed lines to distinguish from primary data path

References restructured into three tiers:
1. Primary/Vendor: Dropbox official blog posts (Magic Pocket,
   Nucleus, Edgestore), Google Drive manage-uploads + manage-
   revisions docs, Microsoft Cloud Files API + File Provider
   Framework, Evernote sync/data-structure developer docs,
   OneDrive Files On-Demand
2. Foundational Papers: Google File System (2003), Rabin
   Fingerprint, rsync technical report
3. System Design References: system-design-primer, InfoQ analysis

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Frame: Infrastructure economics — fork solvency + memory GC

- Fork Solvency Audits: five-question protocol for retiring insolvent timelines
- Memory Garbage Collection: recency, reference, and salience strategies for forgetting
- Queue refreshed with 3 new adjacent frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add article: Specs = Tests for Terraform AI development

New article covering spec-driven Terraform IaC with AI agents.
Core thesis: specs should be expressed as executable tests, not
documents, because tests are deterministically enforceable.

Covers Terraform testing gap (native test framework, Terratest,
policy-as-code with OPA/Sentinel/Checkov), plan-based testing,
terraform-compliance BDD approach, and the three-pillar enforcement
model for agentic DevOps applied to infrastructure code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve generic 'not yet supported' transpiler error messages (#147)

* Initial plan

* Improve generic transpiler error messages with C#-level guidance

Replace generic "The IL opcode 'X' is not yet supported" messages with
specific guidance about what C# pattern caused the error and what to
use instead. Covers common cases like long/double types, boxing, new
objects, virtual calls, throw/try-catch, array .Length, negation,
bitwise NOT, checked arithmetic, and comparison-as-value.

Add GetUnsupportedOpcodeMessage helper and unit tests.

Make error messages type-agnostic: Neg now suggests '0 - x' with
explicit cast examples, Not now qualifies the 0xFF mask as byte-only.

Implement XDG shell protocol (Phase 1.1e)

- Add xdg_wm_base interface for window management base protocol
- Add xdg_surface for surface role assignment
- Add xdg_toplevel for top-level window management
- Support window properties (title, app ID, min/max size)
- Support window state changes (maximize, fullscreen, minimize)
- Handle configure events from compositor
- Auto-respond to ping events for liveness checking
- Add comprehensive test coverage (16 test functions)

Metrics:
- Complexity: max cyclomatic = 5, cognitive = 5 (well below threshold of 9)
- Documentation coverage: 100%
- Package coupling: 0.5 (target < 0.7)
- Code duplication: 0%

All acceptance criteria met per PLAN.md Step 5.

commit.message:
Fix diagram accuracy gaps in chat system design

Addresses three issues flagged in review:

1. Group fan-out-on-write diagram: now includes conversation
   sequencer assigning seq_no, client_message_id for dedup,
   and a clarification note about omitted dedup/epoch validation

2. E2E encryption diagram: split into two separate phases —
   Phase 1 (key exchange via Key Server) and Phase 2 (message
   delivery via normal Messaging Service path). The original
   incorrectly showed the Key Server forwarding encrypted
   messages, conflating two distinct server paths.

3. Added clarification notes to simplified diagrams (fan-out
   on read, group fan-out on write) stating what is intentionally
   omitted so readers don't over-interpret them as full protocol flows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use poke/peek for scroll in main loop, add missing chr_generic.s

Replace scroll() with poke(0x2005, ...) for actual peek/poke demo.
Use peek(0x2002) each frame to reset PPU latch before scroll writes.
Add chr_generic.s (was missing from sample directory).

* Fix poke() to load runtime local variables instead of constant-folding

poke(addr, localVar) was emitting LDA #0 (initial value) instead of
LDA \ (runtime value). Now detects _lastLoadedLocalIndex and emits
LDA Absolute for local variable arguments.

refactor(tests): improve code quality to achieve pylint 10/10

- Fix import ordering in all test files: move sys.path.insert after stdlib
  imports in conftest.py; remove redundant sys.path manipulation from
  individual test files (conftest.py already handles this via pytest loading
  order)
- Add missing module, class, and method docstrings across all test files
- Remove unused 'import pytest' from test_diagnoser.py and test_utils.py
- Eliminate reimported ApiException in test_endpoints.py
- Fix lines exceeding 100 chars in test_diagnoser.py (lines 37, 66) and
  test_yaml_service.py (line 241)
- Add targeted pylint inline suppressions for intentional patterns:
  protected-access (testing private methods), import-outside-toplevel
  (K8s mock must wrap the import), and redefined-outer-name (pytest fixtures)
- Add module-level redefined-outer-name disable in test files using fixtures

All 70 tests continue to pass. Pylint score: 8.62/10 → 10.00/10.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(fuzzer): normalize #netNNN counter in hmp info network output

QEMU's internal netdev counter increments with every process launch,
causing 'info network' to emit a different '#netNNN:' value per execution.
This made every input containing hmp-info op produce a unique stdout hash,
inflating the corpus with noise just like the timestamp bug.

Add DIRECT_PATTERNS list to normalize_qemu_stdout() that strips digit
sequences immediately following a fixed prefix (no colon/space separator),
normalising '#net141:' → '#net0:' across runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Convert all file storage diagrams to Mermaid

Converted 12 ASCII diagrams to Mermaid:
- High-level architecture: flowchart with color-coded groups
- Client sync engine: flowchart (watcher → chunker → indexer → DB)
- Local file change: sequence diagram
- Chunked upload flow: sequence with parallel chunk uploads
- File download & reassembly: sequence with CDN cache miss
- Deduplication: sequence showing two clients, zero-byte second upload
- Three-tree merge model: graph with colored local/remote/synced trees
- Conflict resolution: sequence with conflicted copy creation
- Notification long polling: sequence with Kafka event trigger
- Multi-device sync: sequence with offline→online reconciliation
- Link revocation: sequence with CDN purge + deny-list
- Deletion lifecycle: stateDiagram-v2 (trash→soft→hard→GC + restore)
- Folder move: flowchart showing O(1) parent_id update
- Magic Pocket architecture: layered graph (metadata→routing→storage)

CDC comparison diagrams kept as ASCII since they show
inline byte-level layout not suited to sequence/flow format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove legacy .Core and default API approval baseline files (#8085)

The API tests use VerifierSettings.UniqueForRuntime(), which maps:
- net48 -> .Net.verified.txt
- net8.0 -> .DotNet.verified.txt

The .Core.verified.txt files (15) were for .NET Core 3.x and the
default .verified.txt files (14) were fallbacks - neither runtime
is targeted by the project anymore.

All 18 API approval tests pass on both net48 and net8.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement Wayland SHM support (Phase 1.1d)

Add full shared memory support for Wayland protocol:
- wl_shm: Global factory for shared memory pools
- wl_shm_pool: Memory pool management with mmap
- wl_buffer: Drawable buffer from pool
- memfd_create syscall wrapper for anonymous shared memory

Implementation:

- 637 LoC of comprehensive tests (18 test functions)
- Zero code duplication
- 100% documentation coverage
- All functions under complexity threshold (max: 7, target: <15)

Testing:
- All tests passing including race detection
- Integration test validates full workflow
- Memory safety verified (mmap/munmap lifecycle)

Metrics validation:
- No complexity regressions
- No duplication introduced
- Documentation coverage maintained at 100%

Closes PLAN.md Step 4 (Phase 1.1d)

commit.message:
Add adaptive payload learning integration to all 31 detection plugins

Each plugin's main scan() method now calls learn_from_failure() just
before returning when no findings are produced. This records scan
failures to the AdaptivePayloadLearner knowledge base, enabling future
scans against the same target to receive mutated/adapted payloads that
may bypass WAFs or input filters.

- Plugins returning 'findings': check 'if not findings'
- Plugins returning deduplicated 'unique'/'unique_findings': check the
  actual returned variable for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Cleanup/remove legacy user card management (#58)

* Remove legacy standalone user_card_management.py

This file was an experimental JSON-based standalone FastAPI app that is no longer needed. The active implementation is in backend/app/routes/user_card_management.py which uses the database-backed architecture with proper service layer separation.

* feat: implement header-based authentication via x-user-id dependency

- Create dependencies/auth.py with get_required_user_id() for header extraction
- Update wallet.py: Replace hardcoded user_id defaults in POST, PATCH, DELETE with header dependency
- Update user_profile.py: Replace hardcoded user_id=1 with header dependency
- Follows team decision (March 4, 2026) to use x-user-id header for user context
- Removes TODO comments and fixes critical bugs where endpoints ignored user context
- Maintains verification queries in service layer for security (not redundant)
- Closes gap where wallet.py endpoints were not respecting authenticated user

* refactor: consolidate auth dependency into user_service

Per team's March 4 meeting decision to minimize file sprawl and consolidate services:
- Moved get_required_user_id() dependency from new auth.py into user_service.py
- Updated wallet.py to import get_required_user_id from user_service (not auth)
- Updated user_profile.py to import get_required_user_id from user_service (not auth)
- Deleted backend/app/dependencies/auth.py (consolidated into service layer)

This aligns with treating UserService as source of truth for user identification.
All 4 previously hardcoded user_id instances now use header-based auth via dependency injection.
Header validation still returns 401 Unauthorized if x-user-id is missing.

* refactor: consolidate header-based auth in user_service and user_card_services

Per team's March 4 decision to consolidate services and minimize file sprawl:

1. Added get_required_user_id() as FastAPI dependency to user_service.py
   - Acts as module-level function for header extraction
   - Raises 401 if x-user-id header missing or invalid
   - Can be imported and used in route handlers: @Depends(get_required_user_id)

2. Enhanced user_card_services.py as consolidated service layer:
   - Added _validate_user_context() helper to enforce header validation at service level
   - All methods now explicitly check for valid user_id, return 401 if missing
   - Updated docstrings to guide route handlers on proper usage pattern
   - Documented that this is single source of truth for card operations (not wallet_service.py)
   - Eliminated ambiguity about header validation responsibility

3. Service now consolidates logic from wallet_service.py JSON operations
   - All card operations point to database via CardService
   - All profile operations use UserService (database-backed)
   - No dependencies on legacy JSON-based wallet_service

Key improvements:
- Single point of header validation in service layer
- Clear documentation for route handlers on dependency injection pattern
- Maintains separation of concerns (routes handle HTTP, services handle business logic)
- Ready for route handlers to use: user_id: str = Depends(get_required_user_id)
- Wallet.py not modified per constraints; focused only on service consolidation

* refactor: consolidate header-based auth into user_card_services.py

Per refined requirements to keep all logic within assigned file scope:

1. Reverted user_service.py to original state
   - Removed FastAPI imports (Header, HTTPException, status)
   - Removed get_required_user_id function
   - Restored file to pre-auth implementation state

2. Relocated get_required_user_id to user_card_services.py
   - Added as module-level async dependency function
   - Includes FastAPI Header extraction
   - Raises 401 Unauthorized if x-user-id missing/invalid
   - Can be imported: from app.services.user_card_services import get_required_user_id

3. Updated user_profile.py imports
   - Changed: from app.services.user_service import get_required_user_id
   - To:      from app.services.user_card_services import get_required_user_id

4. Service-level validation maintained in user_card_services.py
   - _validate_user_context() helper enforces 401 errors
   - All methods validate user_id from header
   - Single source of truth for card/profile operations

Result: All header-based identification logic confined to user_card_services.py
Wallet.py remains unmodified per constraints.

* refactor: remove unused wallet module and legacy references

* test: scope default pytest collection to maintained backend suite

* Initial plan

* fix: address PR review comments - whitespace validation and user ID normalization

Co-authored-by: wenxuanalex <82212458+wenxuanalex@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

0.1.1: Add URL crawl-scope filter (#6)

* Initial plan

* Add URL filter module for over-crawl prevention

Add site2skill/url_filter.py with is_url_allowed() function that:
- Restricts crawl scope to descendants of the starting URL
- Rejects URLs with only localization query params (hl, lang, locale)
- Allows URLs with content-switching query params (version, tab, etc.)
- Supports custom excluded query key sets for future configurability

Add test_url_filter.py with 22 tests covering all specified scenarios.

Convert all remaining sequence diagrams to Mermaid

Converted 8 more ASCII diagrams to Mermaid sequence diagrams:
- Service discovery: register → heartbeat → crash → re-route
- Group fan-out on write: sender → group service → per-member routing
- Group fan-out on read: store once, read on demand
- Presence heartbeat: heartbeat → TTL refresh → expiry
- Receipt ack flow: SENT → DELIVERED → READ transitions
- Media upload: pre-signed URL → direct S3 upload → CDN delivery
- Push notifications: Kafka → push service → APNs/FCM
- Exactly-once delivery: retry with dedup check
- E2E encryption: Signal key exchange (X3DH)

All sequence diagrams now render with GitHub-native Mermaid.
Connection state machine and presence subscription box kept as
ASCII since they aren't sequence flows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: recorder captures window context at click time, fixes scroll coalescing

Bug fixes in recorder.rs:
- Window context (title, position, size) now captured in the rdev callback
  at the moment of click/scroll, not during post-hoc consolidation
- Scroll coalescing respects time gaps: only merges within 200ms
- Mouse position tracked via shared last_mouse_{x,y} instead of scanning
  the event list backwards (eliminates (0,0) fallback for first click)
- Listener thread documented as idle-but-alive after stop (rdev::listen
  cannot be cancelled; callback becomes a no-op via stop_flag)
- Added tests: scroll_not_coalesced_across_time_gap,
  mouse_click_captures_window_context
- All 50 Rust + 54 Playwright tests pass, zero warnings

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7554)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter/lint boundary: preserve standalone /// @tag lines verbatim

The formatter was silently applying content rewrites to standalone triple-slash
doc-tag lines (e.g. `/// @func`, `/// @desc`, `/// @return`, `/// @arg`) via
`Core.formatLineComment` → `tryFormatExistingDocComment` → `applyJsDocReplacements`.

This violated the formatter/linter split contract (target-state.md §2.2, §3.2):
  - Tag-alias normalization (@func → @function, @desc → @description, etc.)
  - Parameter-list stripping (/// @function name(args) → name)
  - Type annotation canonicalization

are owned exclusively by `gml/normalize-doc-comments` in @gml-modules/lint.

Fix: add `isDocTagLine(rawText)` as the first guard in
`shouldPreserveRawFormatterLineComment` so every `/// @tag` line is
returned verbatim by the formatter.

Adds a boundary test covering the four key alias forms (@func, @desc,
@return, @arg) as standalone comments.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Step 3: Core Wayland Objects (Phase 1.1c)

Add internal/wayland/client package with support for core Wayland objects:
- wl_display: connection management, sync, registry creation
- wl_registry: global object discovery and binding
- wl_compositor: surface factory
- wl_surface: drawable surface with attach/damage/commit
- wl_callback: synchronization callbacks

Implementation details:
- 907 LoC total (615 implementation + 292 tests)
- Package cohesion score: 1.7 (target: >0.5)
- Zero circular dependencies
- 100% documentation coverage
- All tests passing

Extended wire package with EncodeMessage and Argument.Size methods
for complete message encoding support.

Extended socket package with Send and SendWithFDs convenience methods.

Validation:
- All Go tests pass (make test-go)
- Static build verified (make build)
- Zero code duplication
- Max cyclomatic complexity in client pkg: 10.1 (sendRequest)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add adaptive payload learning integration to all 24 exploit plugins

For each plugin in scanner/plugins/exploits/:
- Add guarded AdaptivePayloadLearner import at module level (after logger)
- Add adaptive learning block in execute_attack() before final return:
  when initial attack fails and _adaptive_learner is present, calls
  self.learn_and_adapt() and attaches adapted_payloads_available +
  adaptive_learning=True to the result dict

No existing logic was modified or removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

   red=storage, teal=async)

would add complexity without clarity.

commit.message:
fix(avatar): resolve "blob already exists" error on avatar upload (#32)

* Initial plan

* Fix avatar upload: gracefully handle unsupported image formats in sharp

When sharp cannot process an uploaded image (e.g., unsupported format),
fall back to using the original uploaded blob URL instead of crashing.
Also add fetch response status check before processing.

Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add logging when sharp image conversion fails

Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use userId for avatar filename with allowOverwrite to avoid blob conflicts

Use deterministic filename `avatars/{userId}.webp` instead of random UUID,
and set allowOverwrite: true with addRandomSuffix: false to handle
re-uploads without "blob already exists" errors.

Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use userId-based pathname and allowOverwrite for initial blob upload

The "blob already exists" error was happening during the initial
client-side upload, not the server-side WebP conversion. Fix by:
- Adding allowOverwrite: true and addRandomSuffix: false to onBeforeGenerateToken
- Using avatars/{userId}.{ext} as the client upload pathname
- Passing user.id to ProfileForm for deterministic naming

Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use MIME type for safe extension instead of filename

Derive file extension from MIME type using an allowlist instead of
extracting from the user-provided filename to prevent extension
manipulation.

Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
